title: Ipanema

(Portuguese pronunciation: [[ipaˈnẽmɐ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) is a neighbourhood located in the [South Zone](/wiki/South_Zone_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "South Zone (Rio de Janeiro)") of the city of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil, between [Leblon](/wiki/Leblon,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Leblon, Rio de Janeiro") and [Arpoador](/wiki/Arpoador "Arpoador"). The beach at Ipanema became known internationally with the popularity of the [bossa nova](/wiki/Bossa_nova "Bossa nova") song, "[The Girl from Ipanema](/wiki/The_Girl_from_Ipanema "The Girl from Ipanema")" ("*Garota de Ipanema*"), written by [Antônio Carlos Jobim](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Carlos_Jobim "Antônio Carlos Jobim") and [Vinícius de Moraes](/wiki/Vin%C3%ADcius_de_Moraes "Vinícius de Moraes"). It borders the neighborhoods of [Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Copacabana, Rio de Janeiro"), [Leblon](/wiki/Leblon "Leblon"), and [Lagoa](/wiki/Lagoa,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Lagoa, Rio de Janeiro").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ipanema%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ipanema%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ipanema%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ipanema%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This article is about the neighbourhood in Rio de Janeiro. For other uses, see [Ipanema (disambiguation)](/wiki/Ipanema_(disambiguation) "Ipanema (disambiguation)").

Neighbourhood in Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

**Ipanema** (Portuguese pronunciation: [[ipaˈnẽmɐ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) is a neighbourhood located in the [South Zone](/wiki/South_Zone_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "South Zone (Rio de Janeiro)") of the city of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil, between [Leblon](/wiki/Leblon,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Leblon, Rio de Janeiro") and [Arpoador](/wiki/Arpoador "Arpoador"). The beach at Ipanema became known internationally with the popularity of the [bossa nova](/wiki/Bossa_nova "Bossa nova") song, "[The Girl from Ipanema](/wiki/The_Girl_from_Ipanema "The Girl from Ipanema")" ("*Garota de Ipanema*"), written by [Antônio Carlos Jobim](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Carlos_Jobim "Antônio Carlos Jobim") and [Vinícius de Moraes](/wiki/Vin%C3%ADcius_de_Moraes "Vinícius de Moraes"). It borders the neighborhoods of [Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Copacabana, Rio de Janeiro"), [Leblon](/wiki/Leblon "Leblon"), and [Lagoa](/wiki/Lagoa,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Lagoa, Rio de Janeiro").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ipanema&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The name *Ipanema* originally referred to a river in the state of São Paulo, its etymology deriving from the [Tupi language](/wiki/Tupi_language "Tupi language") words *ipá* (pond) and *nem-a* (stinking).[[2]](#cite_note-GärtnerHundt1999-2) Possible translations for its original meaning are "worthless water", "stinking lake", "turbid water", or "water worthless for human consumption".[[3]](#cite_note-Lage2019-3) The historian [Teodoro Sampaio](/wiki/Teodoro_Fernandes_Sampaio "Teodoro Fernandes Sampaio") translated *Ipanema* as "bad water".[[4]](#cite_note-Sampaio1901-4)

The neighbourhood in Rio de Janeiro was named after [José Antônio Moreira Filho, 2nd Baron of Ipanema](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Ant%C3%B4nio_Moreira_Filho,_2nd_Baron_of_Ipanema "José Antônio Moreira Filho, 2nd Baron of Ipanema"), who in 1883 created the first urban settlement in the region.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The border area between [Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Copacabana, Rio de Janeiro") and Ipanema is known locally as "Copanema".

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ipanema&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Ipanema today consists mostly of land that once belonged to [José Antônio Moreira Filho](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Ant%C3%B4nio_Moreira_Filho,_2nd_Baron_of_Ipanema "José Antônio Moreira Filho, 2nd Baron of Ipanema"), Baron of Ipanema. The name "Ipanema" did not originally refer to the beach, but to the homeland of the baron at [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo_(state) "São Paulo (state)").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Character

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ipanema&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Character")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Ipanema "Special:EditPage/Ipanema") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *Find sources:* ["Ipanema"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Ipanema%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Ipanema%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ipanema%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ipanema%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ipanema%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ipanema%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Ipanema is adjacent to [Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Copacabana (Rio de Janeiro)") and [Leblon](/wiki/Leblon "Leblon") beaches, but it is distinct from its neighbours. It is relatively easy to navigate because the streets are aligned in a grid. Ipanema's beach culture includes surfers and sunbathers who gather daily at the beach. Every Sunday, the roadway closest to the beach is closed to motor vehicles, allowing local residents and tourists to ride bikes, roller skate, skateboard, and walk along the ocean. Ipanema is one of Rio's most expensive districts to live in; private investment has led to the building of world-class restaurants, shops, and cafés.

Ipanema has played a cultural role in Rio de Janeiro since the city's beginning, with its own universities, art galleries, and theatres. It holds a street parade, the [Banda de Ipanema](/wiki/Banda_de_Ipanema "Banda de Ipanema"), during Carnival festivities separate from those of Rio de Janeiro, attracting up to 50,000 people to the streets of Ipanema.

## Beach

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ipanema&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Beach")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Rio_08_2013_Ipanema_beach_near_Posto_9_Km_6913.JPG/250px-Rio_08_2013_Ipanema_beach_near_Posto_9_Km_6913.JPG)](/wiki/File:Rio_08_2013_Ipanema_beach_near_Posto_9_Km_6913.JPG)

Vieira Souto avenue

The beach at Ipanema is known for its elegant development and its social life. Two mountains called the *Dois Irmãos* (Two Brothers) rise at the western end of the beach, which is divided into segments delineated by *postos*, or lifeguard towers. Beer is sold everywhere, along with the traditional *[cachaça](/wiki/Cacha%C3%A7a "Cachaça")*. There are always circles of people playing [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), [volleyball](/wiki/Volleyball "Volleyball"), and [footvolley](/wiki/Footvolley "Footvolley"), a locally invented sport that is a combination of volleyball and football.

In the winter, the [surf](/wiki/Ocean_surface_wave "Ocean surface wave") can reach three metres (nine feet). The water quality varies from clear light-blue water to a more murky green after heavy rains. Constant swells help keep the water clean, and the often treacherous beach break regularly forms surfable barrels. Just west of this colourful section, towards [Leblon, Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Leblon,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Leblon, Rio de Janeiro"), is another popular stretch of sand known as Posto 10, referring to the #10 lifeguard station.

The beach is one of many areas that suffer from the city's poor waste treatment. In its waters, "fecal coliform bacteria sometimes spike at 16 times the Brazilian government's 'satisfactory' level."[[7]](#cite_note-7) Large amounts of pollutants are still dumped into the sea through the nearby [marine outfall](/wiki/Marine_outfall "Marine outfall") pipe, a matter of increasing concern to ecologists.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Beachgoers often applaud the sunset in the summer.[[9]](#cite_note-9) In 2008, the [Travel Channel](/wiki/Travel_Channel "Travel Channel") listed Ipanema Beach as the sexiest beach in the world.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Posto 9

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ipanema&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Posto 9")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/Ipanema_Beach_%284589735501%29.jpg/250px-Ipanema_Beach_%284589735501%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ipanema_Beach_(4589735501).jpg)

Volleyball players in Ipanema.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/1_ipanema_beach_vidigal_sunset.jpg/250px-1_ipanema_beach_vidigal_sunset.jpg)](/wiki/File:1_ipanema_beach_vidigal_sunset.jpg)

Sunset at Ipanema, with Pedra da Gávea mountain, Dois Irmãos mountain and the neighbourhood of [Vidigal](/wiki/Vidigal,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vidigal, Rio de Janeiro") in the background

Posto 9 is the section of the beach around the #9 lifeguard station, across from Joana Angelica Street. Its notoriety began around 1979 when [Fernando Gabeira](/wiki/Fernando_Gabeira "Fernando Gabeira"), later a [federal deputy](/wiki/Chamber_of_Deputies_of_Brazil "Chamber of Deputies of Brazil") for the State of Rio de Janeiro, returned from political exile in France and was photographed there in a thong.[[11]](#cite_note-Ventura2005-11) He had been a member of the leftist urban guerrilla group [MR8](/wiki/MR8 "MR8"), which kidnapped the American ambassador, [Charles Burke Elbrick](/wiki/Charles_Burke_Elbrick "Charles Burke Elbrick"), in 1969 and demanded the release of fifteen political prisoners in exchange for his life.[[12]](#cite_note-Atencio2014-12)[[13]](#cite_note-Skidmore1990-13)

Gabeira became a political celebrity with the publication of a memoir about his experiences as a guerrilla and his years in exile. In 1979, he was photographed wearing a skimpy purple swimsuit at Ipanema, and gave an interview to a gay and lesbian newspaper, inciting rumours that he was gay, which he neither confirmed nor denied. His going to the beach at Posto 9 made it famous throughout the country.[[11]](#cite_note-Ventura2005-11)[[14]](#cite_note-Atencio201439–40-14)

Posto 9 inherited its status as a gathering spot for counter-cultural types from the area near [Farme de Amoedo Street](/wiki/Farme_de_Amoedo_Street "Farme de Amoedo Street"), next to the dunes called *Dunas do Barato* and a pier that was demolished in the 1970s. It has a long history of public cannabis smoking (illegal in Brazil), police raids, and gatherings of left-wing intellectuals. It is still popular with students, artists, actors, and liberal-minded people.

## Feira Hippie de Ipanema

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ipanema&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Feira Hippie de Ipanema")]

A group of [hippies](/wiki/Hippie "Hippie") started a Sunday market in Ipanema in 1968, and the traditional fair continues with over 700 stalls set up at the Feira Hippie de Ipanema (Ipanema Hippie Market).

## Cultural references

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ipanema&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Cultural references")]

Ipanema gained fame with the rise of the popular [bossa nova](/wiki/Bossa_nova "Bossa nova") sound, when residents [Antônio Carlos Jobim](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Carlos_Jobim "Antônio Carlos Jobim") and [Vinicius de Moraes](/wiki/Vinicius_de_Moraes "Vinicius de Moraes") created their ode to the neighbourhood, "[The Girl from Ipanema](/wiki/The_Girl_from_Ipanema "The Girl from Ipanema")". The song was written in 1962, with music by [Jobim](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Carlos_Jobim "Antônio Carlos Jobim") and Portuguese lyrics by [de Moraes](/wiki/Vin%C3%ADcius_de_Moraes "Vinícius de Moraes"); English lyrics were written later by [Norman Gimbel](/wiki/Norman_Gimbel "Norman Gimbel").
